name: Wild in the Capital: Finding Nature in the Heart of the City
description: When was the last time you stopped to listen to a bird call on your morning walk? Or noticed a butterfly lingering on a flowering tree at a traffic signal? In the rush of urban life, nature often slips into the background. Yet it continues to thrive around us, quietly shaping our memories, neighbourhoods, and sense of belonging. This June, join us at Champaca, Vasanth Nagar, for  Wild in the Capital , a special reading and nature journaling session inspired by  Wild Capital: Discovering Nature in Delhi , the acclaimed new book by conservationist and author Neha Sinha. Date: Saturday, 27 June Time: 11:00 AM onwards Venue: Champaca Bookstore, Vasanth Nagar Price: INR 799 (Fully redeemable on the book) In  Wild Capital , Neha takes readers on an intimate journey through Delhi's overlooked wild spaces. Moving through forgotten rivers, ancient groves, bird-filled parks, and hidden ecological histories, she uncovers stories of wildlife, memory, and hope that exist alongside the bustle of city life. The book asks a simple but profound question: what becomes possible when we begin to notice the natural world around us? To celebrate the book, Neha Sinha will be joined by visual communication designer, animator, illustrator, and author Rituparna Sarkar for a morning of reading, observation, and creative exploration. Together, they will guide participants through the practice of nature journaling: observing birds, butterflies, trees, and urban ecosystems, and translating those observations into sketches, notes, memories, and personal records. Whether you're an artist, a reader, a nature enthusiast, or someone simply curious about seeing your city differently, this session offers a chance to slow down and pay attention. Neha Sinha  is an award-winning conservationist, writer, and columnist whose work explores the intersections of wildlife, ecology, culture, and belonging. She is the author of the bestselling  Wild and Wilful: Tales of Fifteen Iconic Indian Species  and writes regularly on nature and conservation. Her latest book,  Wild Capital , is a deeply personal exploration of cities, wildlife, and hope. Rituparna Sarkar  is a visual communication designer, animator, illustrator, and founder of the design studio VISUAL SARKARSM. A graduate of the National Institute of Design, Ahmedabad, she has spent over seventeen years creating visual narratives across television, publishing, and brand communications. Her work combines illustration, storytelling, and a deep love for observation and the handmade. Seats are limited, and we request you to  RSVP here  to save your spot.

name: BOOK TASTING: STORIES AND FLAVOURS FROM NORTHEAST INDIA
description: What if you could  taste   a book before reading it? Not just read the blurb, but actually sample its mood, voice, and texture? Just like a spoonful of soup or a bite of dessert, giving you a sense of what’s to come. Welcome to another edition of  Book Tastin g , where food and literature come together over the course of an evening. This time, we’re exploring Northeast India through books and cuisine. Writing from the region has long held some of the most vital work in the country. Fiction that questions borders and belonging. Poetry that carries grief alongside tenderness and wit. Essays that reshape how we understand memory, land, and history. Many of these voices still remain underread, and this evening is one way to begin paying attention. What’s on the menu? Each course will be paired with a short reading selected by the Champaca team, followed by a guided conversation and time to reflect together. The menu, curated by Roots & Ferns, brings together flavours from Manipur and Nagaland. Here are all the event details: Date: 6 June, Saturday Time: 6:30PM Onwards Venue: Champaca Bookstore, Vasanth Nagar Price: INR 2500 (INR 500 redeemable on books at the store.) We have very limited seating for this event, and request you to  register here  to book your spot. Come with an appetite. Leave with a reading list you’ll want to return to. Please note: Tickets are non-refundable. If you’re unable to attend, they may be transferred to someone else.
